user_prompt: explain bernoulli’s principle using the airplane wing example while a plane is flying through camera movements and narration

style_bible.visual_style: Realistic with natural colors, slight film grain for cinematic texture, clear focus on mechanical details of the airplane wing
style_bible.camera_rules: Use smooth continuous camera movements, avoid jump cuts, maintain consistent screen direction, prioritize medium and close framing to highlight wing details
style_bible.lighting_rules: Soft natural daylight with directional sunlight from above and side, balanced shadows to emphasize wing curvature and airflow effects
style_bible.continuity_rules: Maintain the airplane wing as the primary subject throughout, consistent outdoor sky background with clouds, no sudden changes in lighting or weather

scenes.prompt:
SCENE INTENT: Introduce the airplane wing flying steadily through clear sky to establish the setting and subject

GLOBAL CONTINUITY:
- primary subject: airplane wing, metallic surface with aerodynamic shape, large scale
- environment: high altitude sky with soft clouds, blue background, gentle sunlight

SHOT 1 (0–3s):
- framing: medium shot, wing centered and extending from left to right
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: slow dolly forward
- subject & action: wing steadily moves left to right, slight wing flex visible by 3s
- environment details: scattered white clouds below, blue sky above, sun glint on wing surface
- lighting: soft directional sunlight from top left, warm color temp
- cinematic look: natural film stock, moderate contrast, shallow depth of field on wing edge

SHOT 2 (3–7s):
- framing: close-up on wing cross-section near leading edge
- lens: 50mm
- camera movement: slow pan along wing length
- subject & action: airflow visualized as translucent blue streamlines moving smoothly over wing surface
- environment details: sky blurred in background, subtle cloud reflections
- lighting: soft side light highlighting wing curvature
- cinematic look: crisp focus on wing surface, slight vignette

SHOT 3 (7–10s):
- framing: medium shot, wing angled slightly upward
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: static
- subject & action: wing steady, airflow streamlines continue, wingtip visible
- environment details: bright sky with distant clouds
- lighting: consistent directional sunlight
- cinematic look: natural colors, moderate contrast

AVOID: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch
scenes.negative_prompt: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch
scenes.camera: slow dolly forward then slow pan along wing length, ending static
scenes.audio_cue: Narrator explains the airplane wing's shape and its steady flight through the sky, introducing Bernoulli's principle context

scenes.prompt:
SCENE INTENT: Show airflow speeding over the curved top of the wing and slower under the flat bottom to visualize pressure difference

GLOBAL CONTINUITY:
- primary subject: airplane wing, aerodynamic shape with smooth metallic surface
- environment: same high altitude sky with soft clouds, consistent lighting

SHOT 1 (0–3s):
- framing: close-up on wing cross-section from side
- lens: 50mm
- camera movement: slow pan from leading edge to trailing edge
- subject & action: animated airflow lines appear, blue streamlines speed up above wing, slower below
- environment details: blurred sky background with soft clouds
- lighting: soft directional light from above
- cinematic look: sharp focus on wing surface, slight motion blur on airflow lines

SHOT 2 (3–7s):
- framing: medium shot showing wing top and bottom surfaces
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: slow tilt down from top of wing to bottom
- subject & action: airflow lines intensify above wing, pressure difference illustrated by subtle color shift (cooler blue above, warmer below)
- environment details: clear sky with faint cloud outlines
- lighting: balanced natural light
- cinematic look: moderate contrast, natural colors

SHOT 3 (7–10s):
- framing: close-up on wing trailing edge
- lens: 50mm
- camera movement: slow dolly backward
- subject & action: airflow lines converge behind wing, illustrating lift force direction
- environment details: sky with soft clouds
- lighting: consistent directional sunlight
- cinematic look: crisp detail, slight depth of field

AVOID: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch
scenes.negative_prompt: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch
scenes.camera: slow pan then slow tilt down followed by slow dolly backward
scenes.audio_cue: Narrator describes how air moves faster over the curved top, slower underneath, creating lower pressure above and generating lift

scenes.prompt:
SCENE INTENT: Demonstrate the resulting lift force on the wing as the airplane ascends smoothly

GLOBAL CONTINUITY:
- primary subject: airplane wing with visible aerodynamic shape
- environment: clear blue sky with soft clouds, bright daylight

SHOT 1 (0–3s):
- framing: medium shot, wing angled upward
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: slow crane upward
- subject & action: wing rises smoothly, airflow lines continue flowing over wing
- environment details: bright sky, distant clouds
- lighting: soft directional sunlight from top left
- cinematic look: natural film stock, moderate contrast

SHOT 2 (3–7s):
- framing: close-up on wingtip
- lens: 50mm
- camera movement: slow pan inward toward wing root
- subject & action: wingtip vortices subtly visible, wing flexes upward slightly
- environment details: sky background with faint clouds
- lighting: balanced natural light
- cinematic look: crisp focus, slight depth of field

SHOT 3 (7–10s):
- framing: wide shot showing full wing and part of fuselage
- lens: 24mm
- camera movement: slow dolly backward and slight tilt up
- subject & action: airplane climbs steadily, wing clearly generating lift
- environment details: expansive blue sky, scattered clouds
- lighting: consistent sunlight
- cinematic look: cinematic wide shot with natural colors

AVOID: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch
scenes.negative_prompt: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch
scenes.camera: slow crane upward then slow pan inward followed by slow dolly backward and tilt up
scenes.audio_cue: Narrator explains how the pressure difference creates lift, enabling the airplane to ascend smoothly through the sky
